assumptions: The question concerns designation in consolidated financial statements, not in separate or individual financial statements.
The declared intragroup dividend has created an intercompany monetary receivable/payable denominated in a foreign currency.
No facts indicate that the dividend balance is part of a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation.

approaches.label: General prohibition on intragroup hedged items
approaches.reasoning: In consolidated financial statements, hedged items must generally relate to parties external to the reporting entity. Ordinary intragroup balances are therefore not designated unless a specific exception applies.
approaches.practical_implication: Start from non-eligibility for an intragroup dividend receivable in consolidation.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.5 says only assets, liabilities, firm commitments or highly probable forecast transactions with an external party can be hedged items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 provides a specific exception for the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item when the related FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The item is a monetary receivable/payable between group entities
The counterparties have different functional currencies
The foreign currency risk results in FX gains or losses not fully eliminated on consolidation
The hedge is formally designated and documented and meets hedge effectiveness requirements
approaches.practical_implication: A recognized intragroup dividend receivable can be designated only for its FX risk if the consolidation exception is met.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 allows the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated.
Paragraph 6.4.1 requires formal designation, documentation and satisfaction of hedge effectiveness criteria.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.reasoning: This is a separate exception for highly probable forecast intragroup transactions; it does not fit a dividend already recognized as an intercompany receivable.
conditions: The transaction is highly probable and still forecast
It is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into it
Its foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss
approaches.practical_implication: If the dividend is already booked as a receivable, this forecast-transaction route is not the relevant hedge model.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 permits certain highly probable forecast intragroup transactions to qualify in consolidated financial statements.
Paragraphs B6.3.5-B6.3.6 require the FX risk of the forecast intragroup transaction to affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge in a foreign operation
approaches.reasoning: A net investment hedge is a distinct hedge type for the foreign currency risk of net assets in a foreign operation, not for an ordinary intragroup dividend receivable.
conditions: The hedged item is an amount of net assets in a foreign operation
The designated risk is foreign currency risk arising from that net investment
The relationship satisfies IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements
approaches.practical_implication: Do not justify hedging an intragroup dividend receivable by analogising to net investment hedge rules.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.5.2 identifies a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting category.
Paragraphs 7, 10-14 explain the scope and mechanics of net investment hedges, including eligible risk and where the hedging instrument can be held.
Paragraph 8 states that the Interpretation applies only to hedges of net investments in foreign operations and should not be applied by analogy to other hedge types.
